notes: All transfer applicants must satisfy University of California admissions eligibility requirements and campus admission selection criteria. All admission requirements must be completed by the end of spring prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility requirements and admission to UC Santa Cruz, please visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that ensures your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

The UC Santa Cruz Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and A-Level examination credit policies may be found in the UC Santa Cruz General Catalog . AP, IBH, and A-level exams may or may not satisfy course requirements, depending on the specific exam.

This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. Although not required for admission, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division preparatory courses as possible from among those listed in the “Recommended Preparation” section before transferring to UC Santa Cruz. Doing so will help you make timely progress toward degree completion. Additionally, completing the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (IGETC) is encouraged, especially for non-screening majors in the Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences Divisions. While not required for admission, IGETC completion will facilitate timely graduation. For more information on incorporating IGETC into your academic plan, please consult with your community college counselor.

All transfer students are strongly encouraged to email music@ucsc.edu to schedule a phone or zoom advising appointment with the undergraduate advisor prior to application. PREPARATION FOR THE MAJOR Although not required for admission, transfer students are encouraged to complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring: MUSC 30A – Theory, Literature, and Musicianship I MUSC 30B – Theory, Literature, and Musicianship II MUSC 30C – Theory, Literature, and Musicianship III Six quarters of ensembles (each course may be repeated to count towards the six) Refer to the "Recommended Preparation" section below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. Voice students should also complete one introductory college-level language course each in Italian, French, and German. THEORY PLACEMENT EXAM Transfer students should take the Theory Placement Exam before the start of fall quarter. Students can also take the examination prior to applying to UC Santa Cruz as a practice guide for future preparation, or in the spring quarter before their first fall quarter. Students can also visit the Music Department website to view the sample examination . The Theory Placement Exam is held twice a year: on the Tuesday before instruction begins, from 10:00 a.m.-12:30 p.m. in the Music Center Recital Hall, and right before the beginning of winter quarter (schedule TBA). No signup is necessary. Students should bring a pencil. Transfer students are strongly recommended to take some music theory courses before transfer to UC Santa Cruz, which generally enables them to test into MUSC 30A – Theory, Literature, and Musicianship I, in their first quarter. Transfer students can also take MUSC 14 – Beginning Western Theory and Musicianship, when offered during Summer Session at UCSC prior to fall of their arrival. Taking MUSC 14 will help prepare students for the Theory Placement Exam and generally results in students placing into MUSC 30A. Admission to MUSC 30A is primarily based on the Theory Placement Exam scores, but students who get a final grade of “A” or "A+" in MUSC 14 are eligible to automatically place into MUSC 30A. Additional Information for Transfer Although not required for admission, students are also encouraged to complete the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (IGETC) , or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before matriculation to ensure timely graduation. For more information on incorporating IGETC into your academic plan, please consult with your community college counselor. Please visit the Music Department website to learn more about this major.
